symptoms: Temperature zone not reaching setpoint
Uneven parison thickness
Burnt smell or visible scorching on barrel
probable_causes: End-of-life burnout due to continuous high-temperature operation
Moisture ingress or short circuit in heating band connection
Thermocouple failure causing runaway temperature
immediate_action: Stop machine, isolate power to affected zone, identify failed band using resistance check, replace heating element before restarting.
prevention_check: Quarterly heating element resistance check (MP007); daily heating band inspection (MP003)

symptoms: Oil puddle under machine
Clamping force loss or inconsistent clamping
Hydraulic pressure drop alarm
probable_causes: Worn or damaged hydraulic seals and O-rings
Cracked hydraulic hose or fitting
Hydraulic pump seal failure
immediate_action: Stop machine immediately, contain oil spill with absorbent material, identify leak source, replace damaged seal or hose before restart.
prevention_check: Quarterly hydraulic oil change and filter replacement (MP006); visual inspection during daily lubrication (MP002)

symptoms: No material flow or reduced flow from die head
Uneven parison or parison with holes
Increased motor amperage
probable_causes: Carbonised/degraded plastic blocking die orifice
Foreign material contamination in HDPE feedstock
Incorrect purge during colour/material changeover
immediate_action: Reduce temperature, attempt purge cycle with virgin material. If blockage persists, stop and dismantle die head for manual cleaning.
prevention_check: Daily die head cleaning (MP001); monthly die head dismantling and cleaning (MP005)

symptoms: Inconsistent neck finish or thread profile
Flash on bottle neck or mouth area
Air leakage during blow moulding cycle
probable_causes: Abrasive wear from long production runs
Misalignment of blow pin to die head
Running with contaminated material causing scoring
immediate_action: Measure blow pin dimensions against drawing tolerance. If out of spec, replace blow pin before continuing production.

symptoms: Blow air not activating or not releasing
Clamping sequence not executing correctly
PLC alarm for valve fault
probable_causes: Coil burnout due to voltage spike or continuous energisation
Contaminated compressed air causing spool blockage
Mechanical wear of valve spool and seals
immediate_action: Check PLC fault code, test solenoid coil continuity. If coil failed, replace solenoid coil or valve assembly.
prevention_check: Annual overhaul includes solenoid valve inspection (MP018); ensure compressed air filter/dryer is maintained

symptoms: Bottles exiting mould with distortion or soft walls
Cooling water temperature rising above setpoint
Reduced cooling water flow rate
probable_causes: Blocked cooling water channels in mould due to scale build-up
Cooling water pump failure
Chiller or cooling tower fault
immediate_action: Check cooling water flow and temperature. If pump failed, switch to backup if available. Stop production if bottle quality is unacceptable.
prevention_check: Annual overhaul includes cooling circuit flush (MP026); monthly mould water channel check

symptoms: Machine stops unexpectedly with PLC alarm
Erratic cycle sequence or missed steps
Indicator lights or HMI display malfunction
probable_causes: Power supply fluctuation or voltage spike
Loose electrical connection due to vibration
PLC I/O module or communication card failure
immediate_action: Record PLC fault code, check E-stop and safety relay status. Inspect main electrical cabinet for loose connections or blown fuses. Contact electrical technician.
prevention_check: Annual electrical panel inspection during overhaul (MP026); ensure voltage stabiliser/UPS is operational

symptoms: Flash on parting line of bottle
Incomplete mould closure
Clamping force alarm on PLC
probable_causes: Worn tie bar bushes or clamping platens
Hydraulic clamping cylinder seal failure
Mould alignment out of spec due to wear
immediate_action: Stop production, inspect mould parting faces and clamping mechanism. Check hydraulic clamping pressure. Adjust or replace worn components.
prevention_check: Weekly bolt and fastener tightening (MP022); quarterly hydraulic oil change (MP024)

symptoms: Reduced output rate at same screw speed
Inconsistent parison weight
Increased melt temperature variation
probable_causes: Abrasive wear from filled or contaminated material
Running at excessively low barrel temperatures causing high screw torque
Extended running hours beyond recommended service interval
immediate_action: Monitor output rate and parison weight closely. If variation exceeds tolerance, schedule screw and barrel inspection at next maintenance window.
prevention_check: Annual overhaul includes extruder screw and barrel inspection (MP034)

symptoms: Temperature zone not reaching setpoint
Cold spots visible on barrel surface
Higher motor load due to unmelted material
probable_causes: End-of-life burnout
Moisture ingress in connection terminal
Overcurrent due to power supply issues
immediate_action: Stop machine, isolate power to affected zone, replace heating element.
prevention_check: Quarterly heating element check (MP007 equivalent for EQ004); daily heating band inspection (MP029)

symptoms: No hydraulic pressure build-up
Excessive noise from hydraulic power unit
Machine cycle not executing
probable_causes: Pump wear due to running with contaminated or degraded hydraulic oil
Cavitation from low oil level
Pump coupling or motor failure
immediate_action: Stop machine immediately, check hydraulic oil level, listen for abnormal pump noise. Do not restart until root cause identified. Replace pump if required.
prevention_check: Quarterly hydraulic oil and filter change (MP006); maintain correct oil level daily

symptoms: No material or reduced flow from die
Parison breaks or has voids
Motor draws high current
probable_causes: Degraded material carbonising in die
Extended idle time at high temperature without purging
Material contamination
immediate_action: Attempt controlled purge at elevated temperature. If purge fails, cool down and dismantle die head for manual cleaning.
prevention_check: Daily die head cleaning (MP010); monthly die head dismantling (MP014)
